intro: A quieter news day: the biggest mover was Alibaba's Qwen3.8-Max preview, while GitHub shipped a cluster of AI-code-review and supply-chain-security updates.

items.headline: GitHub Code Quality reaches general availability, bundling AI code review into a paid product
items.summary: GitHub Code Quality moved from preview to general availability on GitHub Enterprise Cloud and Team, pairing CodeQL's deterministic analysis with AI-assisted detection and Copilot Autofix, plus org-wide dashboards and coverage-based quality gates.
items.why: It's priced at $10 per active committer/month plus usage-based AI charges — the clearest sign yet that GitHub is monetizing AI-assisted review at scale, no Copilot subscription required.

items.headline: Copilot code review now reads instructions from your feature branch, adds CLAUDE.md/GEMINI.md support
items.summary: GitHub updated Copilot code review to pull custom instructions from a pull request's head branch instead of its base branch, and to automatically pick up REVIEW.md, GEMINI.md, and CLAUDE.md files alongside its own config format.
items.why: Lets teams iterate on review guidelines in a feature branch, and standardizes on the config files rival agent tools already use.

items.headline: Anaconda acquires Kilo Code, the open-source agentic coding platform used by 3M+ developers
items.summary: Anaconda acquired Kilo Code, a model-agnostic agentic engineering platform that orchestrates roughly 10 trillion tokens a month across VS Code, JetBrains, web, and CLI, aiming to pair it with Anaconda's enterprise governance and cost-visibility tooling.
items.why: Consolidation move as enterprises struggle to track AI token spend scattered across tools and personal accounts.

items.headline: Alibaba previews Qwen3.8-Max, a 2.4-trillion-parameter model it claims trails only Claude Fable 5
items.summary: Alibaba unveiled a preview of Qwen3.8-Max, a 2.4T-parameter multimodal model handling text, images, video, and documents with a 1M-token context window, days after Moonshot AI's Kimi K3 launch. Alibaba shares rose as much as 5.4% on the announcement.
items.why: No independent benchmark (Artificial Analysis, LMArena) has scored it yet, but it underscores how fast Chinese frontier labs are closing the gap — and open weights are promised soon.

items.headline: China approves Apple Intelligence for launch, built on Alibaba's Qwen models
items.summary: The Cyberspace Administration of China approved Apple Intelligence for release in the country, with Alibaba's Qwen models integrated into iOS, iPadOS, macOS, and visionOS to power on-device text and image understanding and generation.
items.why: Ties Apple's AI rollout directly to a domestic Chinese model rather than a Western one, a regulatory condition unique to that market.

items.headline: Future of Life Institute's Summer 2026 AI Safety Index: no lab scores above a C+
items.summary: FLI's latest safety index graded nine AI labs across 37 indicators; Anthropic led at C+ (2.66), followed by OpenAI and Google DeepMind at C, while xAI, DeepSeek, and Mistral scored F. Reviewers flagged a broad retreat from earlier safety pledges and a pivot toward military AI applications.
items.why: Existential-safety scoring was the weakest domain industry-wide, with no company exceeding a C-.

items.headline: GitHub's 'pwn request' fix for actions/checkout now enforced on every supported major version
items.summary: GitHub's backport blocking pull_request_target and workflow_run workflows from checking out unreviewed fork PR code — previously opt-in via actions/checkout v7 — became mandatory across v2 through v6 on July 20, closing a pattern behind multiple past supply-chain incidents.
items.why: Workflows pinned to a floating tag like @v4 pick up the protection automatically; those pinned to a SHA or exact version need to upgrade manually.

items.headline: GitHub Projects ships advanced search with AND/OR filtering, generally available
items.summary: GitHub Projects' filter bar now supports typed AND/OR search expressions for building custom views, along with a new reviews: filter for pull requests and a 90-day retention policy for deployment statuses.

items.headline: Bitcoin hits one-month high near $65,700, then pulls back with stocks
items.summary: Bitcoin briefly rallied to $65,700 Monday after reports that Iranian mediators proposed a 10-day ceasefire easing oil prices, before giving back gains as 10-year Treasury yields rose to 4.59% and rate-hike expectations firmed, closing near $65,000.
items.why: Shows bitcoin still trading as a macro-risk asset, moving with oil and rates rather than on crypto-specific news.

items.headline: Spot bitcoin ETFs pull in $273M over two weeks — a fraction of the prior $8B exodus
items.summary: US spot bitcoin ETFs took in roughly $273 million over the past two weeks, following an eight-week stretch in which investors pulled more than $8 billion from the funds. Analysts note the recovery barely exceeds the smallest single-week outflow from that slump.
items.why: Too small to support claims of a institutional rotation back into bitcoin just yet.

items.headline: Standard Chartered reaffirms $100,000 bitcoin target, calls MicroStrategy selloff 'noise'
items.summary: Standard Chartered's Geoff Kendrick renewed his year-end 2026 bitcoin price target of $100,000, dismissing recent Strategy (formerly MicroStrategy) stock sales as noise rather than a signal of weakening conviction.
